chapters.title: AI is taking over, and the trust in humans is fading
chapters.text:
The world has shifted from an age shaped by human intelligence to an age shaped by artificial intelligence, or AI. Online services that are now woven into daily life come with a long list of demands from sites and apps that refuse to work unless you accept their terms. A basic sign-up wants your date of birth, full name, location, password, and a valid address. The world is moving toward an era in which human labor is no longer needed, at least according to the CEOs of the top tech firms, who say the future belongs to AI. A computer steered by AI is faster, smoother, and cheaper to run than a team of people. That is why tech leaders like Tim Cook and Mark Zuckerberg have said they prefer machines to people.

Nothing lasts forever, so be ready to change as the world changes.

Since the rise of online transactions and the need to keep information safe on the move, security has been the top concern of every online business. Strong security needs many things, and the unfortunate truth for humans is that tech firms trust machines more than they trust us to handle it.

In this summary, you will learn why the age of Google is slowly fading and what to expect as the post-Google age begins. You will also see why Cryptocosm will be central in the era ahead.

The economy has reached a point where, in principle, it can produce enough for everyone. So the new period we are entering is less about how much is produced and more about how the share gets distributed.

chapters.title: Google is becoming the Alpha of the internet, and everyone else is chasing
chapters.text:
Google is one of the top five fastest-growing companies that form the information oligopoly on the internet. But, unlike the era of Isaac Newton, when money still held clear value for the work it represented, Google is struggling to build the holy trinity of one universe, one money, and one shared belief. Under Newton's view, money was meant to be made of gold coins, and people trusted that their work carried real weight. The fear of counterfeits was almost zero. Today's global system places power in the hands of states and pulls it away from the people. Money has no fixed value because it shifts, changes form, and rises with inflation that reflects the currency itself, not the real value of the work behind it. Still, of all the big companies that dominate information, Google is the largest in terms of gathering and combining data from many sources. As a result, Google holds the big data of knowledge, spanning science, commerce, religion, philosophy, economics, and the study of knowledge itself.

Google makes money by giving things away and selling ads.

From the start, Google was built to be more than software. That is why it pulled in the efforts of some of the sharpest tech minds of the time, including John Doerr and John McCarthy. Google has digitized a large slice of human life through its growing database. It runs search through Google Search, photos through Google Photos, and music and video through YouTube. The remarkable part is that these services are free for users, yet Google is valued at almost 800 billion dollars, only 100 billion behind Apple. By drawing almost everyone to its search engine, where it seems like everything is free, Google has set the stage for a flood of ads. So, instead of paying directly for what we do on Google, we sit through advertising.

chapters.title: Cryptocosm is the next level of security, and it is a real threat to Google
chapters.text:
Cryptocosm describes the web of organizations powered by blockchain technology, where safety and decentralization are the bedrock. Cryptocosm runs on a chain that has no single server and answers to no individual. Online exchanges are not finalized unless both sides confirm them, and money is returned to each party when the deal falls apart. Ten guiding laws sum up why this matters. Safety comes first. Centralized control is dangerous. Protection lasts only as long as the aim holds. Do not be tricked, nothing in this world is free. Time is the deepest measure of cost. Stable money keeps people dignified and in charge. The asymmetry law reminds us that small choices can have outsized effects. Use private key rules to protect yourself. Your keys live with you, not with any government or with Google. Behind every key, public or private, stands a person.

Dignity will soon sit at the heart of cyberspace, and the people will run cryptocosm, not the platforms.

Google today runs top down. The era after Google will run bottom up. Right now, Google holds our data and uses it as it pleases. In the near future, that data will live with you, and you will be able to set a price for it. Most of us have grown tired of the ad barrage that meets us whenever we open a Google page. Soon, we will see ads on our own schedule, and we will even earn a share of the money that comes from our time and attention.

A quick note. Research from the New York Digital Investment Group puts the count of Americans who own at least a slice of Bitcoin at roughly 46 million.

chapters.title: Google's data center power grab, and the rise of AI
chapters.text:
To manage the speed and scale of building a new online world, Google set up a data center in The Dalles, Oregon, in 2005. By 2001, that center was already pushing data at 640 gigabytes per second. Almost a decade later, Google was handling 8.4 terabytes per second, running about 3.5 billion searches a day and 1.5 trillion a year.

That scale pushed Google to branch out into new products, and the move gave us Google Docs, Google Translate, Gmail, and Maps. Many people see this expansion as the future of the internet. Jaron Lanier, often called the father of virtual reality, has a different name for these centers. He calls them Siren Servers, after the bird-women of Greek myth whose songs drew sailors onto the rocks. He believes Google's expansion will be its undoing.

AI dominance over humans is a story that will never play out, because AI lacks emotional depth.

The fear of AI dominance is real among people, and it is growing. Many believe AI will replace human workers in the near future. Worse, the pioneers of big tech in Silicon Valley share the same worry. In 2017, a gathering of tech leaders met in California to talk about the risks of AI's growth.

The general belief among these leaders is that AI will dominate the future, and people should learn to live with it. Yet, ever since AI machines first appeared, humans have worried that AI could one day push them aside, given its high intelligence and sharp accuracy.

AI is programmed to look like a threat to human dominance. These systems are made of code written by people. A computer cannot just come up with new things on its own. It has to be programmed to do so. AI will only be dangerous if it can, through the data it has been fed, start to control humans on its own. As Kurt Godel showed, however, there is no perfectly complete logical system.

chapters.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum are central to the fight against Google's grip
chapters.text:
The Thiel Foundation is a fund set up to give money to young people who drop out of school. It was started by Peter Thiel in 2005. In 2014, the Thiel Fellowship gave Vitalik Buterin, a college dropout, the funds to build Ethereum, a blockchain platform that could bring back the Newtonian idea of money holding real value. In 2009, an unknown person or group using the name Satoshi Nakamoto announced the first release of Bitcoin, a new electronic cash system that runs on a peer-to-peer network to prevent double-spending. It is fully decentralized, with no server and no central authority. The peer-to-peer network gives each user a set of keys tied to their account. Those keys encrypt messages, and only the two people in a transaction can unlock them. With Bitcoin, the blockchain does not share personal information between users. Transactions are only confirmed once both sides have agreed.

It is not enough to stop a problem. The goal is to prevent it from happening again.

A new block is created at regular intervals. The block logs the most recent activity of the network. A block's timestamp is created through mining. A new block appears once a problem has been solved by the combined power of many high-end computers. When mining is complete, new bitcoins are generated, and the process starts again. Bitcoin moves quickly across platforms. Every transaction is recorded in a block, and each block is connected to a chain. The blockchain is public, so it is easy to follow a bitcoin's path back to its origin. Beyond security and easy access, Bitcoin has created many jobs, apps, and firms. For instance, mining equipment companies, businesses that build machines for the extraction of ores, fossil fuels, minerals, and similar commodities, brought in 500 million dollars on their own. Bitcoin and Ethereum are shaping up to be the future of online transactions.

chapters.title: Blockchain is the closest thing to a new system of real money
chapters.text:
When Australian computer scientist Craig Steven Wright came forward in 2016 with the claim that he was the anonymous Satoshi Nakamoto, one of the first people to call out the claim as false was Vitalik Buterin. Buterin is the founder of Ethereum, a blockchain app built to handle smart contracts and cryptocurrencies safely.

Ethereum launched in 2015 with the goal of giving people a secure place to exchange contracts and run transactions. Currencies, assets, and shares move through the blockchain, and until both sides confirm the exchange, the transaction is not processed, and both sides are refunded their share. Like Bitcoin, Ethereum has its own coin, which has been valued at almost 60 billion dollars.

The rise of Cryptocosm has also lifted another company, Blockstack, led by computer scientist Muneeb Ali. Blockstack is another platform that runs on blockchain. Unlike Bitcoin and Ethereum, Blockstack is a browsable network and an open-source space that developers and users can dig into.

Cryptocosm lowers the chance of fraud, because users cannot change their passwords without the approval of a third party.

While startups raise money to build the next generation of fast, low-power chips, Google is still expanding in a way that demands more power. The future of tech is moving away from bulky machines toward small, fast hardware that does more and costs less. Nvidia, a Silicon Valley giant, is leaving behind the old hot chips and moving toward faster, more efficient designs. Led by Bill Dally, Nvidia is one of the many companies working to shrink physical scale while making their products more efficient and faster. Dally has pointed to a future where graphics processors run smoothly and move data with no delay. In what looks like a slow but steady and efficient takeover, blockchain appears to be here to stay, and that does not bode well for Google.

chapters.title: Final thoughts
chapters.text:
Innovations and ideas grow out of a careful look at problems and how best to solve them. As Daniel Berninger, the founder of the Voice Communication Exchange Committee, put it, people do not know what they want, but the moment they get what they need, that need turns into a want. Google's dominance is the result of its expansion, and its downfall may be the result of the same expansion. The internet has run the human world since the late 2000s, and there is almost nothing that cannot be done online now. The fear of artificial intelligence is a story that will not come true unless, somehow, machines begin to think on their own. Machines are the product of programs and commands written by humans. They may be faster, more efficient, and even more accurate than we are, but there is no way they can replace us.

Blockchain has given new life to the idea of a system that mirrors the Newtonian idea of currency from the 18th century. With that rise, Google's way of doing business is at risk of becoming outdated and getting pushed out of the market. Cryptocosm has its flaws. The value of gold is predictable, and it is already used as a token of exchange, but its price still moves with inflation that cannot be fully controlled. If those flaws are addressed over time, Google will have no choice but to compete again in the market, or risk losing what it has built.

Try this

There are many platforms online where you can buy Bitcoin. Before signing up with any of them, talk to someone who has a deep understanding of how the market works. Trading in an online market takes more than theory. It takes experience.
